stories.title: India ‘cockroach’ activist ends hunger strike after 26 days as protests spread
stories.summary: Sonam Wangchuk halts strike after receiving assurances parliament will address protesters’ concerns about exam system The Indian “cockroach” activist Sonam Wangchuk has ended a 26-day hunger strike over an exam paper scandal that has spiralled into a wide-ranging, youth-led wave of demonstrations against Narendra Modi’s government. Supporters, meanwhile, have called a nationwide day of protest on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Masked men invading and taking Amazon communities ‘hostage’ in organised crime surge, say local leaders
stories.summary: Delegation sent to Brasília after gunmen from organised crime group enter homes in western Amazon Indigenous leaders on the Brazilian-Peruvian border have said their communities are being “taken hostage” by organised crime groups who are running rampant across the region, destroying rainforest, building roads and trafficking drugs. Earlier this month, masked men armed with semi-automatic weapons entered homes in Apiwtxa, a village in the Kampa do Rio Amônia Indigenous territory, intimidating peo

stories.title: Burnham urged to award £2.4bn military satellite contract to Airbus over US rival
stories.summary: Unite union leader says giving Skynet 6 WSS contract to Lockheed Martin would be a ‘massive mistake’ Business live – latest updates One of the Labour party’s biggest union backers has called for Andy Burnham to award a £2.4bn military satellite contract to the European manufacturer Airbus rather than a US rival, Lockheed Martin, amid concerns that as many as 600 jobs may be at risk. Sharon Graham, the general secretary of the Unite union, said it would be a “massive mistake” to go for a US compa

stories.title: Canadian government urged to block data-sharing deal between Thomson Reuters and US ICE
stories.summary: ICE will pay Canadian media giant US$125m to use its investigative database to identify ‘immigration fraud’ A Canadian federal opposition party leader is calling on the Mark Carney government to block the latest data-sharing deal between US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and the Canadian media giant Thomson Reuters. Avi Lewis, the federal leader of the New Democratic party (NDP), demanded on Tuesday that Ottawa address the contract and further restrict Canadian corporations from doing

stories.title: EU signals relief over new US tariffs as Trump respects transatlantic truce
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Union on Friday signaled it could live with new U.S. tariffs imposed over its alleged failure to ban imports made with forced labor after President Donald Trump largely stuck to the terms of a transatlantic trade truce. Washington on Thursday slapped new double-digit tariffs on dozens of U.S. trading partners, including the European Union, scrambling to rebuild its “tariff wall” which was struck down by the Supreme Court in February. EU exports will be subject to a 10 per

stories.title: China strikes back at EU sanctions targeting defense, tech firms
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — China announced it will add export restrictions on 14 EU defense and technology companies after the EU added 14 Chinese businesses to its Russia sanctions lists. This marks Beijing’s most direct and forceful response yet to EU sanctions listings, which it denounced as “egregious.” Recent studies estimate that up to 90 percent of sanctioned goods still reaching Russia transit through China. Germany’s rapidly expanding defense company Rheinmetall is among 14 firms that Chinese exporters

stories.title: Ukrainian drone strike ignites major blaze at Saint Petersburg warehouses
stories.summary: A Ukrainian drone strike sparked a major fire at a Wildberries warehouse near Saint Petersburg early on Thursday 24 July, sending thick smoke across the city and temporarily disrupting flights at Pulkovo Airport, according to Russian authorities.

stories.title: Julian Leeser says his son no longer feels safe at Jewish events – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australian exports sent to the US will be hit with a 12.5% tariff as the US president imposes levies on more than 80 nations. The Trump administration unveiled the latest tranche of tariffs for countries over their “failure to impose and effectively enforce a prohibition on the importation of goods produced with forced labour”. This, in our view, makes no sense. Forced labour is definitely something which needs

stories.title: University courses covering Israel-Palestine should be audited, Julian Leeser tells royal commission
stories.summary: Inquiry’s focus this week is on security for the Jewish community, with the MP telling the commissioner his son is afraid to attend synagogues and events Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The shadow education minister, Julian Leeser, has called for universities to undertake an audit of any classes that teach about the Jewish community, Israel or Palestine. Appearing before the royal commission into antisemitism and social cohesion on Friday, Leeser said he had been “ve

stories.title: Texas nun detained by ICE speaks out for first time: ‘It was very heartbreaking for me’
stories.summary: Sister Leticia Ugboaja says she went into a cold sweat as ICE officers approached her in McAllen on her way to church A nun who was taken into immigration custody last month in Texas said on Thursday that she went into a cold sweat as two armed men approached her while she walked to Sunday mass in her habit, thinking about the day’s Bible readings and carrying little more than her rosary and cellphone. “Honestly, in that moment, I did not understand what was going on, what was happening to me,”

stories.title: Maga influencer Laura Loomer reverses course on Ukraine after Kyiv visit
stories.summary: Rightwing provocateur meets Volodymyr Zelenskyy, who says he expects to meet Donald Trump in Washington next week Volodymyr Zelenskyy will meet Donald Trump in Washington next week, according to the rightwing US influencer Laura Loomer, amid signs the White House’s traditional hostility towards Ukraine is beginning to crumble. In an interview with Loomer, the Ukraine president said he will probably attend the funeral of Senator Lindsey Graham and hold talks with Trump, which will take place as t

stories.title: Trump’s new tariffs ‘a sales tax that will make life harder for Americans’, say Democrats – US politics live
stories.summary: Even some Republicans apparently expressing concern over measures against 85 countries What to know about Trump’s new tariffs on more than 80 countries Sign up for the US Breaking News email It’s worth noting that a Harris Poll survey for the Guardian found earlier this year that 70% of Americans have reported paying more due to tariffs , and a majority of voters, including 60% of Republicans, say tariffs have had a negative effect on consumers. Some Republicans applauded Trump’s latest announce

stories.title: Four men in 20s die after car crashes into lamp-post in Merseyside
stories.summary: Police appeal for witnesses after collision in Huyton leaves four men dead and a fifth in hospital Four men in their 20s have died after a car crash in Merseyside in the early hours of Friday. Police were called just before 2am to reports that a black Seat Cupra had left the road and collided with a lamp-post in Huyton. The car was travelling from Liverpool towards Knowsley. Continue reading...

stories.title: Judge orders Hegseth to explain US military’s new testosterone policy
stories.summary: Court questions plan to offer male hormone treatment to cisgender troops while denying it to trans service members Pete Hegseth’s controversial plan to screen older soldiers for testosterone deficiencies and offer them more of the hormone faced scrutiny this week from a federal judge, who ordered the Trump administration to explain a discrepancy with the treatment of transgender troops. The US defense secretary announced earlier this month that male service members aged 30 and above would be che

stories.title: Justice Elena Kagan decries idea that US supreme court ‘rubber-stamps’ Trump’s agenda
stories.summary: Kagan rejects notion that high court ‘does whatever the administration wants’, pointing to tariffs ruling and more Liberal justice Elena Kagan has said it was “wrong” to accuse the US supreme court of becoming a rubber stamp for Donald Trump in his second term, amid criticism of its authorization of some of the president’s most controversial actions. Pointing to rulings against the president on key issues such as tariffs, Kagan – one of three liberals on a court that has in recent years been def

stories.title: Uzbek economy grows 8.5% as services and investment expand
stories.summary: Official figures presented at a presidential review showed investment reaching €24.6 billion and exports €12.7 billion in the first half of 2026, as Moody's upgraded Uzbekistan's sovereign credit rating and Fitch shifted its outlook to positive.

stories.title: ‘We’re getting into an ugly phase’: Trump’s new tariffs are angering voters and US allies alike
stories.summary: Tariffs are back — and they couldn’t come at a worse time for the GOP. Some Republicans fear the eye-popping new levies President Donald Trump threatened to slap on Canada and generic drug makers this week will aggravate Americans who were already blaming the tariffs for stubborn inflation ahead of a midterm election  dominated by economic issues . The president has long brandished tariffs as a cudgel in negotiations and some industry insiders see the promise of a future 50 percen

stories.title: Polish conservatives split ahead of 2027 election
stories.summary: WARSAW — The nationalist Law and Justice (PiS) party that ruled Poland from 2015 to 2023 split on Friday in a political rupture that undermines the conservative camp’s attempts to topple pro-EU Prime Minister Donald Tusk in an election next year. After the victory of conservative Karol Nawrocki in the presidential election of May 2025, PiS hoped it would build momentum toward a return to government in 2027. That prospect raised alarm in Brussels given PiS’s long track record of confr

stories.title: Mass job cuts loom at VW as profits fall steeply on China sales slump
stories.summary: Carmaker expects to sell 3% fewer vehicles this year as it pushes through a programme that could eliminate 100,000 roles Volkswagen has reported a steep fall in profits and cut its revenue forecast amid a sales slump in China, as the German carmaker pushes through a brutal cost-cutting programme that includes axeing up to 100,000 jobs. VW said it expects sales revenue to fall by up to 3% this year, a reversal of a previous forecast for a 3% increase on last year’s €321.9bn (£275.3bn), because of

stories.title: France orders total evacuation of Cap Ferret peninsula as wildfire spreads
stories.summary: ‘Intense and highly unpredictable’ blaze forces more than 40,000 people to flee popular holiday spot Europe live – latest updates Authorities in south-western France have ordered the total evacuation of the Cap Ferret peninsula on the Atlantic coast by land and sea as an “intense and highly unpredictable” wildfire advances on one of the country’s best-known holiday spots. Spain’s government has also taken the exceptional step of declaring a national emergency as a series of out-of-control wildfi

stories.title: Burnham urged to lobby EU leaders directly to waive EES border controls
stories.summary: Lib Dems call on PM to push for suspension of ‘bungled, malfunctioning’ system as overseas holiday travel peaks Andy Burnham has been urged to lobby EU leaders directly to suspend the new entry-exit system, EES, as the peak weekend for overseas holidays begins. About 2.2 million British holidaymakers are expected to head abroad between Friday and Monday, the majority to Europe, with all schools in the UK now on their summer break. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine’s pivot to warehouse strikes brings war closer to ordinary Russians
stories.summary: Kyiv’s attacks on Wildberries, country’s answer to Amazon, destroy goods made, sold, and bought by everyday people Standing in front of a vast warehouse engulfed in flames outside Moscow, Tatyana Mikhailova watched as her entire stock of 4,000 fur coats, bought months in advance for the winter season, burned. “It’s not just a warehouse that’s burning,” Mikhailova said in a video she shared on Instagram. “It’s the time you’ve invested, the immense stress you’ve endured, your money, and your last

stories.title: The latest US troops killed in Trump’s Iran war are remembered for lives of service
stories.summary: Angel Rampersad, Michael Swinton, Isabella Gonzales and Tyler Feehan were among 18 US troops killed in the war A 30-year-old husband and father of two. A 25-year-old officer just weeks away from earning his MBA. A 19-year-old recent high school graduate. And a 28-year-old from Queens, New York, with a “radiant smile”. The Pentagon this week identified three American service members who were killed, and a fourth believed to have been killed, in the Middle East over the past week amid an escalatio

stories.title: Four Palestinians and one Israeli killed during West Bank settler attack
stories.summary: Several others seriously injured in attack that residents of Palestinian village say also involved Israeli military Four Palestinians and one Israeli settler have been killed in a shooting in the northern West Bank, health officials have said, during what residents described as a settler attack also involving the Israeli military. Several more people were seriously injured. The serious incident in the Palestinian village of Tell, near Nablus, is the deadliest attack in the West Bank this year an

stories.title: Poland’s main rightwing opposition splits after loyalty pledge ultimatum
stories.summary: Former prime minister Mateusz Morawiecki and allies say they were forced out over factional disagreements Poland’s main opposition Law and Justice (PiS) party has split, after former prime minister Mateusz Morawiecki and his allies said they were forced out over factional disagreements on policy direction ahead of next year’s parliamentary election. Speaking at a press conference, the party’s longtime leader , Jarosław Kaczyński, said dozens of members of parliament effectively left the party af

stories.title: Southport killer Axel Rudakubana is manipulating system, victims’ families say
stories.summary: Rudakubana moved to psychiatric hospital after it was concluded he could not be safely managed in prison The Southport killer Axel Rudakubana has been accused of attempting to “manipulate the system” by the families of his victims, after it emerged he had been transferred from prison to a psychiatric hospital. Rudakubana, 19, was jailed for life in January last year, with a minimum sentence of 52 years, for the murders of Elsie Dot Stancombe, seven, Bebe King, six, and nine-year-old Alice da Sil

stories.title: France evacuates Cap Ferret peninsula as Spanish wildfires merge near Madrid – Europe live
stories.summary: French evacuees using hundreds of mobilised boats or the only road linking the area with the mainland as evacuation also ordered in areas near Spanish capital Over in France, it’s not just the south-west that gets affected, but the country’s south-east regions are also being hard hit, AFP noted. Dozens of fires flared up and are being fanned by the mistral wind around the historic village of Cotignac – officials saying around 25 houses had been destroyed since Tuesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Andy Burnham spends first working day at new No 10 North in Manchester – UK politics live
stories.summary: PM, on his first day at new base, says it will create ‘that sense of a more balanced country, that big things can be done from here’ Andy Burnham has arrived by police convoy at No 10 North at Heron House in Manchester: Andy Burnham was probably feeling left out of the “Celtic alliance”, after apparently telling John Swinney that Scottish independence was “off limits”. Continue reading...

stories.title: Wildfire emergency near Madrid forces over 10,000 to flee as Spain sounds alarm
stories.summary: Civil Guard officers, firefighters and the Military Emergency Unit battle fierce wildfires near Madrid, with more than 10,000 people evacuated amid extreme heat.

stories.title: 'All paths appear closed': Iranians weigh prospect of US invasion of their country
stories.summary: Euronews spoke to five Iranians about whether US boots on Iranian soil would mean liberation or catastrophe. Their answers revealed a country that has run out of easy answers after nearly 50 years of the Islamic Republic and months of war.

stories.title: Armoured fighters storm Denmark’s Spøttrup Castle for IMCF world championship
stories.summary: Nearly 600 armoured fighters from 30 countries have gathered at Denmark's Spøttrup Castle for the International Medieval Combat Federation (IMCF) World Championship, turning one of Scandinavia's best-preserved medieval fortresses into an arena for full-contact historical combat.

stories.title: Netanyahu orders 'firm' military action after deadly West Bank clash
stories.summary: Netanyahu said he had ordered the IDF to "accelerate the legalisation of existing farms and the establishment of new ones" after the incident around the village of Tell.

stories.title: Middle East crisis is ‘getting out of control’, UN chief warns – as it happened

stories.title: Adelaide airport pulls ads from conservation groups but keeps gas and mining ads during ALP conference
stories.summary: Digital billboards from environmental groups, targeting Labor MPs attending national conference, cancelled with airport stating they are ‘too political’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Conservation groups have questioned why their advertisements targeting Labor politicians were pulled by Adelaide airport for breaking rules on political advertising while others promoting gas and mining kept running. stories.summary: Australia’s trade minister, Don Farrell, says tariff regime is unjustified and inconsistent with free trade agreement with US Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Donald Trump has hit Australian exports with new trade tariffs, claiming the country is not doing enough to combat forced labour, but the Albanese government has demanded he remove them immediately. The United States announced on Friday morning (AEST) that m

stories.title: Donald Trump says decision on ‘massive attack’ against Iran is close
stories.summary: President’s remarks follow Houthi strikes on Saudi tankers in the Red Sea and oil prices rising past $100 a barrel again Donald Trump has said he is close to deciding whether to launch “a massive attack” against Iran, on a scale larger than strikes already witnessed during the months-long conflict with Tehran. Speaking to the Axios news site, the US president said: “I am considering a massive attack. Bigger than ever before. I am close to making a decision. We are all set for it.” Continue readi

stories.title: Once a textile powerhouse, Syria is betting on an industrial comeback
stories.summary: Syria once produced garments that reached Arab and European markets, but the civil war dismantled the industry over the years. With sanctions lifted and investors beginning to return, Aleppo's first major trade fair in years is testing whether that world can be rebuilt.

stories.title: Modi adresses India's youth's 'Cockroach' movement as protesters hold capital sit-in
stories.summary: The "Cockroach" protests which have gained a wider following from Indians of all ages over government accountability, unemployment and economic opportunity is considered one of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government's biggest challenge yet.

stories.title: ‘Cockroach’ protests: India’s Modi promises to punish officials over exam leaks
stories.summary: PM says ‘those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared’ as scandal spirals into widespread anger Narendra Modi has vowed to set up “fast-track courts” to speed up the punishment of officials accused of leaking examination papers, as the Indian prime minister seeks to quell a youth-led “cockroach” protest movement that has rocked Delhi and posed one of the biggest challenges to his government. In his first public remarks on the movement, Modi posted on X: “Nothing is more impor

stories.title: Merz: Linnemann folgt „meiner Bitte“ und wird neuer Gesundheitsminister
stories.summary: BERLIN – Kanzler Friedrich Merz hat den Personalwechsel an der Spitze des Gesundheitsministeriums am Freitag vor Reportern bestätigt. Carsten Linnemann wird neuer Gesundheitsminister, die bisherige Amtsinhaberin Nina Warken wechselt ins Kanzleramt. Damit hat Merz der Kabinettsumbau offiziell gemacht, der mit dem Rücktritt von Jens Spahn als Vorsitzender der CDU/CSU-Bundestagsfraktion begann. Der bisherige Kanzleramtschef Thorsten Frei folgt auf Spahn, Warken wiederum folgt auf Frei. Linnemann, d

stories.title: Ireland urged to put pressure on Germany over pro-Palestinian activist’s trial
stories.summary: Irish politicians concerned about Daniel Tatlow-Devally’s treatment in jail want Micheál Martin to send observers to Ulm 5 trial A cross-party delegation of Irish politicians has urged Dublin to send observers to a high-security trial of pro-Palestinian activists in Germany and to raise concerns about the Irish defendant’s detention when the German chancellor visits next week. Mairéad Farrell of Sinn Féin, a member of the seven-member delegation, said she and her colleagues had attended the hear

stories.title: For outraged Germans, the Jens Spahn scandal is about more than his surrogate baby | Tania Roettger
stories.summary: The powerful politician was no stranger to controversy, but brazenly defying a ban on surrogacy that he had long defended was the last straw Up until last week, Jens Spahn was, by all accounts, a model of political success in Germany. At just 22, he was elected to the Bundestag for the conservative Christian Democrats (CDU), and rose steadily through the ranks of the party. He was a leading official in the finance ministry, led the ministry of health through Covid and, in 2025, became his party’

stories.title: Australian regulator TGA warns of ‘rare but severe’ eye disorder linked to GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic
stories.summary: TGA says product information for some diabetes and weight loss drugs updated with warning of NAION disorder Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia’s medicines regulator has issued warnings for several type 2 diabetes and obesity-related treatments – including Ozempic and Mounjaro – over the potential to cause a “rare but severe” eye disorder that could lead to blindness. The Therapeutic Goods Administration’s

stories.title: Weather tracker: More than 900 wildfires rage across Canada
stories.summary: Fires remain out of control in Northwest Territories and Ontario, while Argentina endures record night-time heat Recent hot, dry weather in Canada has triggered hundreds of wildfires across the country. As of Wednesday, more than 900 were burning, scorching about 3m hectares of land. While fires have broken out in several provinces, the greatest concern is in the Northwest Territories and Ontario , where several remain out of control. Poor air quality has been reported across affected regions, w

stories.title: Two Russian men jailed in Angola for terrorism and spying
stories.summary: The men were convicted over alleged plan to stage coup and organise deadly demonstrations over fuel prices last year Two Russian men have been found guilty by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying after being accused of planning a coup and helping to organise deadly demonstrations in the southern African country last year. Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin were sentenced to eight and 11 years in jail respectively. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in Angola for cultural and busine

stories.title: Israeli security minister joins Jewish extremists as they storm al-Aqsa mosque compound
stories.summary: Far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir was among thousands who gathered at Jerusalem’s most sensitive holy site Israel’s far-right minister of national security, Itamar Ben-Gvir, joined thousands of Jewish extremists who stormed Jerusalem’s most sensitive holy site on Thursday. Escorted by armed police, they prayed, waved the Israeli flag and sang the national anthem in a provocative display of political power. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK ‘ready to defend itself’ after Iranian threat over US bases
stories.summary: IRGC claims base in south-west England was used by US to launch bombing missions, making it ‘legitimate target’ The government has said the UK is “ready to defend itself” after Iran’s military warned that any bases being used by the US were “legitimate targets”. The UK has allowed the US to launch “defensive” operations from British bases hosting American planes since the start of its war on Iran but has refused to help in offensive operations. Continue reading...

stories.title: The ‘cockroaches’ marching on India’s government – podcast
stories.summary: South Asia correspondent Hannah Ellis-Petersen on the Cockroach movement – and the students driving India’s biggest protests in years ‘I’ve never seen this many young people out on the streets,’ says Hannah Ellis-Petersen . For days, tens of thousands of Indians have been marching under the banner of the Cockroach Janta party. Continue reading...

stories.title: Merz ally challenges Germany’s far-right firewall ahead of crucial state elections
stories.summary: BERLIN — A key ally of Friedrich Merz has ignited a major political debate by suggesting the chancellor’s centrist conservatives should break a taboo and cooperate with the country’s most popular party: the far-right Alternative for Germany. Michael Kretschmer, premier of the eastern state of Saxony and a member of the chancellor’s center-right Christian Democratic Union, is challenging Merz’s insistence on keeping the country’s firewall against the surging AfD ahead of crucial elect

stories.title: Trump threatens to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for shipping damage
stories.summary: Tehran said Friday that US President Donald Trump's threat to pay for shipping damage with Iranian assets was an 'incendiary precedent'.

stories.title: France: Massive blaze engulfs Cap Ferret forests as thousands evacuated
stories.summary: A fast-moving wildfire forced the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula after destroying dozens of homes, as 700 firefighters battled to contain the blaze.

stories.title: Trial date set in US drug trafficking case against Nicolas Maduro
stories.summary: The US in April allowed Venezuela to pay for Maduro's and Flores's legal defence, something previously prohibited under US sanctions.

stories.title: Trump threatens to bomb Tehran back for every Iranian attack in Hormuz
stories.summary: The US president said Washington would destroy one piece of civilian infrastructure — including sites near Tehran — each time Iran fires on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, as the war entered its 11th consecutive day of US strikes.

stories.title: Mayor shot dead in Mexico months after surviving assassination attempt
stories.summary: Valentín Lavín Romero is the 14th mayor to be murdered since Claudia Sheinbaum became president in 2024 A mayor was shot dead on Wednesday in the Mexican state of Morelos after having survived a previous assassination attempt earlier this year. He is the 14th mayor to be murdered since Claudia Sheinbaum became president in October 2024. Valentín Lavín Romero, who was from the Green party, an ally of the ruling leftwing Morena party, was wounded at the end of January when hitmen shot him at a pet

stories.title: Liberia seizes €277 million worth of cocaine linked to 'organised and well-funded' trafficking network
stories.summary: According to the Global Initiative Against Transnational Organized Crime, West Africa is a key transit hub for cocaine bound for Europe.

stories.title: Coexistence isn’t a medieval memory. Ask the rabbis of the Muslim world
stories.summary: Rabbi Mendy Chitrik argues that Europe should engage more with Jewish communities in Muslim-majority countries, viewing them as living bridges for dialogue, coexistence and cooperation rather than relics of the past.

stories.title: White House says Trump will not intervene in Tate brothers' extradition to UK
stories.summary: According to US media, Andrew Tate has also sought to build ties with Trump's sons, Donald Trump Jr. and Barron Trump.

stories.title: Canada cancels joint bridge-opening celebration with US amid trade tensions
stories.summary: Trump’s 50% tariff threat means there will be no ribbon-cutting celebration of Gordie Howe International Bridge A planned joint US-Canada ceremony on Friday celebrating the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, is no longer expected to take place after Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods. In announcing the tariffs, the president accused Canada of unfairly discriminating against US automobiles, alcohol and dairy products, e

stories.title: Nobody in Europe knows or cares what Kemi Badenoch thinks. That’s a big mistake | Simon Nixon
stories.summary: The opposition leader wants to pull the UK out of the ECHR, wrecking treaties from the Good Friday agreement to the Gibraltar deal Sitting in Brussels, or one of the EU’s 27 chancelleries, one could be forgiven for tuning out the latest psychodramas in Britain’s Conservative party. After all, the country has a new Labour prime minister who is a pro-European keen to build upon the “reset” begun by Keir Starmer . Who cares what a party in opposition thinks about anything when it is still reeling f

stories.title: MoD cancels army training in Kenya over powers to prosecute British soldiers
stories.summary: September exercise called off after Kenya raises concerns over funding and jurisdiction for criminal offences The British army has been forced to cancel training for its troops in Kenya after the Kenyan government refused to ratify a bilateral defence agreement. While the UK government wanted to renew the existing agreement, Kenyan lawmakers had sought changes to give local authorities greater powers to hold British soldiers accountable for any offences committed in the country. Continue reading

stories.title: Trump links landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia to normalisation with Israel
stories.summary: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and Saudi Minister of Energy Abdulaziz bin Salman formally signed the agreement, along with "bilateral safeguards" against non-proliferation.

stories.title: Trump says he is 'close to deciding' on a 'massive attack' on Iran
stories.summary: Earlier in the day, Trump threatened the Houthis with "major military punishment" after the Tehran-backed militant group in Yemen claimed on Thursday that it had struck two Saudi oil tankers transiting the Red Sea.

stories.title: India’s Cockroach movement protesters show no sign of backing down
stories.summary: Thousands of people again on the streets of Delhi calling for education reforms despite police violence on Monday Taru had come to the protest prepared this time. In one hand was an anti-government poster and in the other was a bicycle helmet. “We need to have protection in case Delhi police beat us up again,” said the 25-year-old, who stood among her friends. “We are not scared of them and we won’t run away, no matter what they do. We’ll stay here till they listen.” On Tuesday, central Delhi st

stories.title: Opposition parties shut down Indian parliament in solidarity with ‘cockroach’ protests
stories.summary: Proceedings suspended as politicians call for Modi government to address police violence and education scandals India’s opposition parties have shut down parliament in a protest calling for the resignation of the education minister, in solidarity with the new youth-led “cockroach” movement . Opposition politicians, led by the Congress party leader Rahul Gandhi, gathered outside the parliament building in Delhi on Wednesday, many dressed in black and waving placards calling on Dharmendra Pradhan

stories.title: Video shows death of man in Italy while under police restraint - video
stories.summary: Warning: some viewers may find the following footage distressing. Italian police have clashed with demonstrators in Bologna, northern Italy, during a protest over a man's death. The demonstrations on Monday night followed Italian media reports that Abderrahim Fakir, a Moroccan-born businessman, had died on Sunday when police tried to arrest him. A video filmed by a resident and shared on social media shows Fakir repeatedly calling for help as two officers sit on him and ambulance workers appear

stories.title: Total solar eclipse books out hotels two years in advance in New Zealand city
stories.summary: Dunedin will be the focal point, having last experienced such an eclipse in 1163 – before any humans reached New Zealand Hotels are booking out two years in advance in the New Zealand city of Dunedin as it prepares for the region’s first total solar eclipse in more than 850 years. At 4.17pm on 22 July 2028, the moon will pass in front of the sun, casting a roughly 100km-wide shadowy band across parts of southern New Zealand. Everywhere within that band – from tourist hotspot Queenstown in the we

stories.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak kills more than 1,000 people
stories.summary: The World Health Organization has said that the true scale of the outbreak could be two to four times larger than official figures indicate.

stories.title: Spanish police evacuate residents as wildfire threatens homes near Madrid
stories.summary: Guardia Civil officers evacuate residents in Villa del Prado, central Spain, as a fast-moving wildfire threatens homes near Toledo with flames and smoke.

stories.title: Divers restore Italy's Christ of the Abyss monument
stories.summary: Divers clean the Christ of the Abyss statue off San Fruttuoso, Italy, preserving the iconic underwater monument placed there in 1954.

stories.title: Gulf oil producers race to build alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz as oil hits $100
stories.summary: Gulf oil producers are spending billions of dollars on pipelines designed to route crude around the Strait of Hormuz, as Iran's grip on the waterway drags on causing oil prices to hit $100 a barrel for the first time since May.

stories.title: Giant swing offers breathtaking ride above the Swiss Alps
stories.summary: A giant swing opens at 2,110 metres on Switzerland's Grand Chamossaire peak, offering tourists an aerial ride above the Alps in Villars.

stories.title: Giant hot dog sculpture returns to New York Times Square
stories.summary: A giant hot dog sculpture returns to New York's Times Square as artists unveil a 65-foot installation marking America's 250th anniversary.

stories.title: Thousands evacuated as wildfire spreads in southwestern France
stories.summary: A wildfire spreads near Le Porge in Gironde, France, as firefighters battle flames across the forest and thousands of people are evacuated.

stories.title: Venezuela earthquake damages add up to almost $20 billion, World Bank says
stories.summary: Almost a month after the devastating earthquakes, Venezuela has a mammoth task ahead in terms of rebuilding, rehabilitating and mitigating the social and economic damage of the biggest natural disaster in decades.

stories.title: Michelin-starred restaurant owner in South Korea faces jail over ant dessert
stories.summary: Prosecutors seek a one-year sentence and heavy fine after chef garnished dish with insects not approved for consumption Prosecutors in South Korea have asked a court to sentence the owner of a Michelin-starred restaurant to one year in prison for serving a dessert topped with ants, an insect not approved for consumption under local food safety law. The request, made on Monday, also sought a 20m won (£10,108) fine for the company that runs the restaurant, Yonhap news agency reported . Continue re

stories.title: Australia to warn China it won’t be bullied by ‘provocative‘ actions as it builds military and nuclear arsenal
stories.summary: Foreign minister Penny Wong will deliver speech criticising China’s recent launch of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Penny Wong will warn China that Australia and other neighbouring nations will not be bullied by its “provocative” actions, after its involvement in another “destabilising and dangerous” incident this week. The foreign affairs minister – who will deliver the address on Thursday in the Philippines, where the 5

stories.title: British woman jailed in Hong Kong for blackmail after accusing banker of rape
stories.summary: Isabel Rose, 26, sentenced to six years for blackmail and perverting course of public justice over her allegations against British man A British woman who reported being raped to police in Hong Kong has been sentenced to six years in prison for blackmail and perverting the course of public justice. Isabel Rose, 26, from south-east London, travelled to Hong Kong in early 2024 to visit a British banker she had met while travelling in south-east Asia. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump promises US support for Lebanon in White House meeting with president
stories.summary: Lebanon and Israel have held rare direct talks mediated by Washington. Aoun’s visit was the first to the White House by a Lebanese president since 2009.

stories.title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
stories.summary: UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus. The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) where the worker had been treating patients with the disease, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
stories.summary: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common. The central Japan c

stories.title: My friends left Ukraine after the Russian invasion. Now I wonder if we live in the same world | Diana Berg
stories.summary: Anger, guilt, shame: the deepest unseen wound of this war is the rift between those who left and those who stayed behind “Can we reschedule?” Over the years of working with western colleagues, I had learned to accept that they don’t fully understand what war feels like from the inside. They would ask me to find someone in occupied Mariupol for an interview, or seem genuinely puzzled that I couldn’t just log on at a time that suited them. I made my peace with this long ago – they don’t have to un

stories.title: Scorched earth and a hungry wasp: photos of the day – Wednesday
stories.summary: The Guardian’s picture editors select photographs from around the world Continue reading...

stories.title: Two trams collide on bridge in Rotterdam, injuring at least 15 people - video
stories.summary: Webcam footage shows the moment two trams collide on the Erasmus Bridge in the Dutch city. Officials said at least 15 people were injured, one seriously, in the collision that occurred on Wednesday Continue reading...

stories.title: Wildfire spreads in southeastern France as firefighters battle blaze
stories.summary: Firefighting aircrafts battled a fast-moving wildfire in southeastern France as the blaze spread across hundreds of hectares, prompting evacuations and threatening nearby communities.

stories.title: Firefighters battle major wildfire as thousands of hectares burn in central Spain
stories.summary: Firefighters battle a major wildfire in Spain's Guadalajara province as flames destroy thousands of hectares and force more than 1,200 people to flee.

stories.title: Russian prison intercepts cat used to smuggle drugs into penal colony
stories.summary: In Russia, offenders reportedly used a cat to smuggle illegal drugs into Correctional Colony No. 17 in the city of Nizhny Novgorod.

stories.title: US appeals court rejects Biden bid to block release of memoir ghostwriter recordings
stories.summary: A divided three-judge panel of the US Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit suspended its decision until 3 August to give Biden more time to consider a further appeal.

stories.title: Aliyev says former Russian and German officials were in Baku for possible meeting over Ukraine
stories.summary: Azerbaijan’s president revealed that former Russian and German officials were in Baku at the same time earlier in July, but that Azerbaijan’s authorities were neither notified nor aware of the meeting, which might have involved discussions over Ukraine.

stories.title: Trump says US has no interest in Iran talks as cost of war increases
stories.summary: The US entered its 11th consecutive night of strikes on Iran, while Tehran expanded its offensive against Gulf states. Meanwhile, Pakistan stepped up diplomatic efforts in hopes of salvaging the interim deal between the two.

stories.title: Outcry in Algeria over doctor deaths after gruelling on-call shifts
stories.summary: A resident neurosurgeon died of a heart attack after a 24-hour shift, and an anaesthesiologist died days later — deaths colleagues say reflect years of chronic overwork, understaffing and inadequate conditions in Algeria's public hospitals, demanding a revision of on-call rules dating back to 2013.

stories.title: Aerial footage reveals the scale of Spain’s La Mierla wildfire
stories.summary: Aerial footage shows the scale of the La Mierla wildfire in Guadalajara, Spain, as firefighters battle a blaze that has burned up to 30,000 hectares.

stories.title: Mamdani says NYC lacks authority to arrest Israeli PM Netanyahu
stories.summary: Netanyahu's office has dubbed the ICC a "kangaroo court" and called the arrest warrant against the Israeli leader "bogus."

stories.title: Zendaya's ancient Iranian earrings stir debate over stolen artefacts
stories.summary: Experts said the Iranian 3,000-year-old gold disc medallions Zendaya wore at a promo event for Christopher Nolan's Odyssey were "likely looted". Separately, a Zoroastrian symbol worn by IShowSpeed at the World Cup final has sparked further conversation about Persian cultural identity.

stories.title: 'It will get worse': World’s second-coldest capital faces record heat
stories.summary: Experts warn that the extreme heat reflects a broader climate trend that is increasing health, water and food security risks across Central Asia.

stories.title: Drones hit Wildberries warehouses in Russia for second week running
stories.summary: Fires broke out at Wildberries warehouse sites in Krasnodar and Stavropol after overnight drone attacks, injuring 15 people and forcing both facilities to suspend operations.

stories.title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
stories.summary: Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials. According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora. Continue reading...

stories.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
stories.summary: Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability. There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the globa

stories.title: What the EU wants from Andy Burnham
stories.summary: European leaders have made positive noises about the new UK PM, but he is likely to face the same restrictions as Starmer Andy Burnham is unknown in Brussels, but that will soon change. In his schedule for the autumn is an EU-UK summit that may underscore the same dilemmas that have confronted previous British prime ministers in the relationship with Europe. EU leaders were quick to offer congratulations to Britain’s prime minister, the seventh since the 2016 Brexit referendum. The European Comm

stories.summary: Officials admit new post-Brexit EES system is ‘not perfect’, as airports and airlines voice fears over delays The EU has rejected calls by airports and airlines to suspend the implementation of new fingerprinting and facial recognition border controls even though it admits there are “20 difficult spots” with queue chaos. With only a week to go before the peak summer holiday season starts, EU officials said the new entry/exit system (EES) was “not perfect” but it would tell travel industry repres

stories.title: Spain and Gibraltar celebrate as border fence falls after signing of ‘historic deal’
stories.summary: Treaty came into effect at midnight, eliminating border controls on land frontier with British overseas territory Europe live – latest updates UK politics live – latest updates Spain and Gibraltar are celebrating the fall of the last frontier fence in western Europe after the signing of a post-Brexit deal that brings an end to border checks for residents, tourists and the thousands of Spanish workers who cross into the British overseas territory every day. The agreement, which was signed in Brus

stories.title: France and UK to increase staffing at border controls in effort to avert travel chaos
stories.summary: Disruption at Channel crossings expected to rise amid new fingerprint and facial recognition checks France and the UK have agreed to increase staffing at border controls in response to warnings of travel chaos caused by new fingerprinting and facial recognition checks. Disruption at Channel crossings is expected to rise sharply next weekend at the start of the summer holiday season, with MPs saying there would be “utter chaos and miles of tailbacks” unless the EU’s entry-exit system (EES) is fix

stories.title: Donate now to a Top 10 CNN Hero
stories.summary: Anderson Cooper explains how you can easily donate to any of the 2021 Top 10 CNN Heroes.

stories.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
stories.summary: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

stories.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
stories.summary: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

stories.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
stories.summary: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

stories.title: CNN Heroes: Sharing the Spotlight
The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
stories.summary: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

stories.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
stories.summary: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

stories.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
stories.summary: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

stories.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
stories.summary: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

stories.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
stories.summary: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

stories.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
stories.summary: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

stories.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
stories.summary: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

stories.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
stories.summary: Paul Templer was living his best life.

stories.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
stories.summary: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

stories.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
stories.summary: This is it.

stories.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
stories.summary: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

stories.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
stories.summary: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

stories.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
stories.summary: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

stories.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
stories.summary: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.
